# UC-17.3.43 · FortiSASE Admin Configuration Audit (Fortinet)

## Description

FortiSASE policies are centrally managed and affect all connected users globally. Configuration audit logs enable change tracking, compliance, and root-cause analysis when policies cause access issues.

## Value

FortiSASE policies are centrally managed and affect all connected users globally. Configuration audit logs enable change tracking, compliance, and root-cause analysis when policies cause access issues.

## Implementation

Forward FortiSASE management audit events. Alert on after-hours changes or bulk policy modifications. Correlate with ITSM change tickets.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-fortinet_fortigate` (FortiSASE admin audit).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=fgt_event` subtype=system, logid related to admin/config.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward FortiSASE management audit events. Alert on after-hours changes or bulk policy modifications. Correlate with ITSM change tickets.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=sase sourcetype="fgt_event" subtype="system" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(msg),"(?i)config|policy|rule|admin.*login|object.*modified")
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last values(msg) as actions by user, ui
| sort -last
```

Understanding this SPL

**FortiSASE Admin Configuration Audit (Fortinet)** — FortiSASE policies are centrally managed and affect all connected users globally. Configuration audit logs enable change tracking, compliance, and root-cause analysis when policies cause access issues.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=fgt_event` subtype=system, logid related to admin/config. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-fortinet_fortigate` (FortiSASE admin audit). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: sase; **sourcetype**: fgt_event. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=sase, sourcetype="fgt_event", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(msg),"(?i)config|policy|rule|admin.*login|object.*modified")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, ui** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with FortiGate/FortiSASE manager for the same policies, users, and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (admin changes), Timeline (configuration events), Bar chart (changes by admin).

## SPL

```spl
index=sase sourcetype="fgt_event" subtype="system" earliest=-30d
| where match(lower(msg),"(?i)config|policy|rule|admin.*login|object.*modified")
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last values(msg) as actions by user, ui
| sort -last
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (admin changes), Timeline (configuration events), Bar chart (changes by admin).
